sense.definition: the state of being present at an event or a place

examples.x: The attendance at the conference exceeded expectations, with over 500 participants from around the world.
She takes attendance at the beginning of each class to track students' participation.
The theater was filled to capacity, with every seat occupied, indicating strong attendance for the performance.
The church saw a decline in attendance after implementing a new schedule for services.
The company encourages regular attendance at team meetings to ensure effective communication and collaboration.

sense.definition: the absence or failure to attend a particular event, meeting, or class

examples.x: The nonattendance of several employees at the mandatory training session raised concerns about their commitment to the company.
Nonattendance at the parent-teacher conference was attributed to conflicting schedules and lack of communication.
The professor recorded the nonattendance of several students in the class register and followed up with them to address any issues.
Nonattendance at the concert was disappointing, but the performers still delivered an exceptional show to those who attended.
The organization implemented a policy to address nonattendance at staff meetings and encourage greater participation among team members.

sense.definition: the habitual or prolonged absence of individuals from their duties or obligations, typically without valid reasons

examples.x: The company's productivity suffered due to high rates of absenteeism among its employees.
The manager implemented measures to address absenteeism in the workplace, such as flexible work arrangements and employee wellness programs.
Chronic absenteeism can have negative consequences on academic performance and social development among students.
The HR department conducted a survey to identify the underlying causes of absenteeism among staff and develop strategies to mitigate its impact.

sense.definition: a formal record or log where specific information, such as names, dates, or transactions, is systematically recorded

examples.x: The librarian maintained a register to keep track of borrowed books and their return dates.
Each student's name was listed in the class register along with attendance records.
The financial accountant meticulously updated the register to document all incoming and outgoing transactions.
The register contained details of all patients seen by the doctor, including their medical history and treatment plans.
In the archive, the register served as a valuable resource for researchers, providing insights into historical events and individuals.

sense.definition: an official record of a student's academic performance, including grades and courses completed

examples.x: Sarah requested her high school transcript to submit with her college applications.
John included his college transcript with his job application to demonstrate his academic achievements.
The university registrar's office processed transcript requests for alumni and current students.
The admissions office reviewed the student's transcript to assess their eligibility for the scholarship.
The employer requested transcripts from the applicants to verify their educational background.

sense.definition: the assignments, projects, and tasks done by students as part of their course of study

examples.x: She spent hours completing her coursework for the biology class, including lab reports and research papers.
The coursework for the computer science course involves programming assignments and algorithm analysis.
Students are expected to submit their coursework by the deadline to receive full credit.
The university requires students to complete a certain number of credit hours of coursework to graduate.
He struggled to balance his coursework with part-time work and extracurricular activities.

sense.definition: the act or process of looking at a written or printed piece and comprehending its meaning

examples.x: Her reading of the novel was interrupted by a loud noise outside.
The child's reading improved significantly after practicing with phonics.
She enjoys spending her evenings curled up with a good book, lost in the pleasure of reading.
The teacher observed the students' reading abilities during the silent reading session.

sense.definition: the activity or skill of making words on paper or a screen to express ideas or information

examples.x: Writing helps you share your thoughts with others.
He spent time practicing his writing every day.
Writing is a skill you improve with practice.
Writing helps organize your ideas.

sense.definition: the action of reading something aloud from memory, especially in public
a visual or oral communication, typically using slides or other visual aids, delivered to an audience to convey information or persuade them to take some action
a talk given to an audience about a particular subject to educate them, particularly at a university or college

examples.x: The lecturer provided handouts to accompany the lecture.
She attended a lecture on modern European history.
The lecture was held in the university’s main auditorium.
The lecture on ancient philosophy lasted for two hours.
The series includes weekly lectures on art and culture.

sense.definition: a class or course at a college or university in which a small group of students and a teacher discuss a specific subject

examples.x: The seminar focused on contemporary issues in international law.
She attended a seminar on environmental sustainability last week.
The students were required to present their research findings at the seminar.
He was excited to participate in the seminar because it allowed for more interactive discussions.
The professor led a seminar on the ethics of artificial intelligence.

sense.definition: a meeting at which some individuals work on a specific project or subject and exchange information about it
a formal and academic conference or seminar

examples.x: The university hosted a colloquium on environmental sustainability, featuring presentations from experts in the field.
Attendance at the colloquium was required for graduate students in the department, as it provided valuable insights into current research trends.
The colloquium served as a platform for scholars to exchange ideas and engage in interdisciplinary dialogue.
The keynote speaker delivered a compelling address at the colloquium, highlighting emerging challenges and opportunities in the field.
Participants at the colloquium were invited to submit papers for consideration in the upcoming academic journal special issue.

sense.definition: a trip made by researchers or students to learn more about something by being close to it

examples.x: The class went on a field trip to the museum to learn about ancient civilizations.
Field trips are an integral part of the curriculum, providing students with hands-on learning experiences.
The teacher organized a field trip to the zoo to study animal behavior and habitats.
The students were excited to explore the national park on their field trip.
Field trips offer students the opportunity to apply what they learn in the classroom to real-world situations.

sense.definition: scientific study or research conducted in the real world and not in a laboratory or class
an activity or program that takes place outside of regular school or work hours, often involving clubs, sports teams, or volunteer organizations

examples.x: She joined the debate club as an extracurricular activity to improve her public speaking skills.
Participating in an extracurricular activity like volunteering can enhance a student's college application.
His favorite extracurricular activity was playing on the school's soccer team.
They encouraged their children to take up an extracurricular activity to develop new interests and skills.
The school offers a variety of extracurricular activities, from drama and music to robotics and community service.

sense.definition: the routine of transporting children to and from school, typically by parents or guardians

examples.x: Every morning, Sarah's mom does the school run before heading to work.
John's dad juggles his work schedule to manage the school run for his children.
The traffic during the school run can get heavy with so many parents dropping off their kids.
Sarah's family carpool with neighbors to make the school run more efficient.
During the pandemic, many parents relied on organized school run services to ensure their children got to school safely.

sense.definition: a task given to a student to do

examples.x: The teacher handed out a challenging assignment on algebraic equations.
Completing the history assignment required researching multiple sources.
She spent hours working on her science assignment, conducting experiments and writing up her findings.
He struggled with the math assignment but sought help from a tutor.
The English assignment involved writing a persuasive essay on a controversial topic.
